subject: Anthropology
title: Organ Transplantation
description: A fast breakdown of Organ Transplantation for builders.

hook.text: You carry a stranger's heart in your chest. Whose person are you now? Organ transplants raise deep cultural questions.

buildup.text: Organ transplants challenge cultural ideas about personhood, death, the body, and the boundaries between self and other.

discovery.text: In many cultures, body parts carry identity. Recipients report feeling changed — sensing the donor's presence within.

twist.text: The global organ trade exploits the poor. Wealthy patients buy organs from desperate sellers in an unequal market.

climax.text: Organ transplants expose how medicine, markets, and meaning collide. The body becomes both gift and commodity.

punchline.text: A transplanted organ carries more than biology.

quiz.question: What cultural issue does organ transplantation raise?
options: Questions about personhood, identity, and the body as commodity
